summary: Plato: Republic VI–VII — Cave allegory + Form of the Good
description:
**Parent:** Corpus Expansion Tier 1 (child 4 of 8) ## Source - **Translator / license:** Jowett, 1871. US-PD. - **URL:** index https://www.sacred-texts.com/cla/plato/rep/index.htm; paginated `repBBSS.htm` (BB=book, SS=section). - Form of the Good = Book VI (`rep0601.htm` confirmed: "idea of good") - Allegory of the Cave = Book VII (`rep0700.htm` confirmed: den/shadows/prisoners/chained) - **Format:** `html` on the targeted Books VI–VII pages only (~4 files). NOT the full Republic via `html_multi` — too much narrative content unrelated to the concept payload. - **Chunking:** `paragraph-group`. These pages use real `

` tags (unlike the single-`

` Plato dialogues), so the structure is cleaner.

## Per-page entries

Probably best as 4 separate `[[source]]` entries (one per VI-VII page), with ids like `plato-republic-6-1`, `plato-republic-7-0`, etc. — matches the Yasna/Upanishads precedent. Implementer's call vs a single `plato-republic-cave-good` with `html_multi`.

## Concept payload

The **Cave** is the canonical cross-tradition illusion/awakening node — wires to Gnostic "sleep," Buddhist ignorance, and Ouspensky's `mechanical_humanity` already in `western_esoteric`. **Form of the Good** sources the highest principle / sun analogy.

## Ref

`docs/corpus-expansion-candidates.md` §3.4

resolution.note: 4 manifest entries (rep0600/01/02 + rep0700) + 4 chunking configs landed; total 37 chunks across the Cave + Form-of-the-Good targeted ingest. Same Jowett/paragraph-group pattern as the other 3 Plato children.
